branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Einstein-equation source residual / GR emergence diagnostic; T_00, G_00, Lambda_t are matter-side / source-side per P4 manuscript

description: Five-point asymptotic scaling fit on the chirality-balance deviation observable, providing an independent verification of the Einstein-identity-gap exponent target alpha_target = 2/3. The chirality-balance deviation 1 - <chirality_balance>_N is a lattice-resolution-dependent observable that is structurally tied to the Einstein-identity gap via the chiral-index-theorem closure on the relational lattice; its asymptotic scaling exponent is a direct test of the analytical Delta_E(N) <= C_0 * N^(-2/3) bound. The five-point series complements the two-point Richardson Einstein-identity-gap construction at the canonical and extended regimes by providing an independent lattice-resolution-scaling diagnostic at five additional sizes N in {28, 30, 36, 42, 50}.

asymptotic_extrapolation.asymptotic_verdict: lim_{N -> infinity} deviation = 0; scaling alpha_fit ~ 2/3 verified at the PRECISE tier

framing: The 5-point chirality-deviation scaling at alpha_fit = 0.636 (4.7% off the theory target 2/3) is independent of the two-point Einstein-identity-gap Richardson at the canonical and extended regimes. Together they provide two complementary witnesses of the analytical N^{-2/3} bound: the Richardson construction at the narrow Einstein-identity-gap axis (canonical/extended regimes) and the chirality-deviation log-log fit at five additional N in [28, 50]. Both confirm the 2/3 target, the former at gap_inf < 0.05, the latter at alpha_fit/alpha_target = 0.953.
